comment_text:
This drama is terrible. The guy, Gwansik, is a good son, good husband, good father - everything. But there's his wife, who is only obsessed with her daughter and completely ignores her son.

I want this drama to be like Reply 1988, where they ignore her daughter but add her screen time more. But in this drama, all the ranting is for their ungrateful daughter, who's only using them. Where was she when her father was in the hospital?

Even Gwansik isn't equal for both their children. If he's a perfect husband, then why isn't he a perfect father for his son? The daughter's scenes are annoying as hell, and there are Instagram edits of her like we're all like her. No, we're not. If we had that much love from our parents, why are you arguing with your parents? Such a brat she is.

This drama is only based on females' delusions. Even in the end scenes, how he's a green flag for her, but it's not shown how he suffers and does everything for them. In every episode, this drama is corny as hell. Solid 3/10. There are only scenes great that show her son-father moments and the negative man who's turning positive - only they bring some charm.

comment_text:
I was deeply touched by this series and felt compelled to leave a review—not just to express my appreciation, but to help you understand if this series is meant for you. Because When Life Gives You Tangerines isn’t just something you watch—it’s something you feel, something you grow through.

I’m not sure why I kept dodging this series for so long, but I’m deeply grateful that my mom recommended it to me. Watching When Life Gives You Tangerines felt less like watching a show and more like gently peeling back layers of life itself. It’s a series that speaks softly, but with the kind of truth that stays with you long after the screen goes dark.

If you’re someone looking for answers, or just trying to understand life a little better—from where you’ve been, where you are, and where you’re headed—this series offers perspective. Whether you're a young adult figuring out your path or a parent trying to make sense of your role, there’s something for everyone here.

As a child, it helps you realise the silent weight our parents often carry—the things they do for us that go unnoticed, the discipline that comes from love, and the sacrifices that aren't always articulated. And as a parent (or someone who one day might be), it gently teaches how to nurture, trust, and show up for your children emotionally, even when life feels heavy.

One of the biggest lessons it left me with is that life doesn’t come with a manual. We often feel pressured to plan everything, but sometimes, flowing with the moment—without resistance or fear—isn’t weakness. It’s wisdom. I used to think “going with the flow” meant lack of control, but now I understand it's about trusting the rhythm of life, tuning into what feels right for you.

I’d recommend watching this series not just from your own lens, but as if you’re a quiet observer—standing a few steps above or beside the characters. Let the story unfold as you take in the nuances of culture, relationships, gender roles, and personal growth. It’s a beautiful mirror of the world we live in, particularly for those of us navigating multiple cultures, generational beliefs, and the evolving definition of self.

That said, I did feel a few things were missing. I would’ve loved to see a deeper portrayal of the sibling dynamics—how brothers and sisters carry unspoken histories and mutual healing. Also, while the series focuses heavily (and beautifully) on the journey of the women, I wished for more emotional depth from the father’s side. His silence, though powerful, left me wondering about the quiet battles he fought—the weight of providing, the emotional restraint expected of men, and how often we take father figures for granted.

In many cultures—including those I’ve grown up around—the woman is often seen as the soul of the home but not always seen for who she truly is. This series, however, shows how much strength comes from a partnership built on mutual support. It reminds us that being present for one another—financially, emotionally, and spiritually—isn't a given; it’s a choice. One that makes or breaks the foundation of a family.

If you’re someone like me—sensitive to emotion, observant of the details, and always trying to find meaning in the world around you—this series will speak straight to your heart. Watch it slowly. Let it linger. It’s not just a story. It’s a life lesson wrapped in tangerines.

comment_text:
I just finally finished this drama and this was the first time I finished a drama without binge-watching it. I found myself watching episodes whenever I could, savoring each moment because I didn't want it to end; I was so invested in every character. It’s hard to put into words, but it felt like I was part of their world, almost like one of their neighbors. How do I move on from this drama? Despite the sad scenes that brought tears to my eyes, my heart felt happy because I witnessed such a beautiful love story. I loved it from the very first episode all the way to the end. It’s a wonderfully realistic slice of life—heartwarming, full of morals, and filled with life realizations.

While not everyone may agree with my perspective, I truly believe that Gwan Sik and Ae-Sun's love story is the greatest love story ever told. Their relationship is so pure, genuine, vocal, innocent, selfless, straightforward, and gentle, it’s simply two souls destined to be together for life. Their bond is so deep and captivating that it's hard to look away from their journey together. Every moment they share is filled with passion and devotion, pulling at the heartstrings. Their relationship is an inspiration, showcasing the power of love in its purest form. I find myself completely immersed in their story, unable to tear myself away from the magic they create together. ❤️ 

The casting and plot were excellent, and I loved how the characters were crafted with such depth. Honestly, I couldn't ask for more from this drama—what I truly crave is to be loved the way Gwan Sik loves Ae-Sun. 🥰

This drama is a masterpiece, excelling in every aspect. Park Bo-gum completely stole my heart! ❤️

comment_text: I have watched many slice of life dramas, movies, shows, all having some eye-opening messages towards life but this is the first drama which has made me relate to the characters, which actually impacted my life and the way I think about life, even if for a few minutes. I don't know why this drama was so different but it was. I am going to go to college in a few months and I was so excited to start a new life, away from my parents with unlimited freedom. But when Geum Myeong was crying for her family in Japan, it immediately made me relate to her and I realised how much I will miss my family when I go to college. I have never cried so much for a drama as much as a cried when the one who shall not be named died. I felt the regret of that missing last hug. I usually avoid such dramas thinking they would have a boring simple plot. But the boring simple plot of this drama is what made me love it. It wasn't a usual whirlwind romance drama where the male lead and the female lead fall in love, face some struggles, get married and then live happily ever after. This was a drama as mundane as daily life, showing the struggles of a regular couple, of a family, of the truth of life. I cried when they cried, I laughed when they laughed, I worried for them when they were worried. Such dramas are rare to come by and I doubt any drama will be able to replace this one anytime soon.

comment_text:
I’ve never been much of a K-drama enthusiast, and truthfully, I don’t watch them often. But this particular series—When Life Gives You Tangerines—left a profound impact on me. It wasn’t just a story; it felt like a quiet, emotional awakening. As someone born in 2005, the historical backdrop and societal circumstances portrayed in the show were completely unfamiliar to me. And yet, there was something deeply human and universally resonant about it.

Through its characters and narrative, the show gave me a glimpse—however shallow—into the lives my parents and grandparents may have lived. The struggles, the unspoken sacrifices, and the quiet resilience of that era, though far removed from my own experiences, suddenly felt relatable. It made me pause and reflect on the paths they've walked, the hardships they endured, and the love they carried without always knowing how to express it.

What struck me most was how beautifully it captured the chaos of life—how people continued to hold onto hope, relationships, and dignity even when everything around them seemed to fall apart. Watching it didn’t just entertain me—it reminded me that behind every generation is a world of quiet storms they’ve weathered, so that we could live the lives we often take for granted. It’s a rare piece of storytelling that lingers in the heart long after the screen fades to black

comment_text:
It was my fate that I got to witness this story. I had been watching it for a few days, and today the series ended. I never cry, but today I did—because this story teaches us so much about life, love, friendship, and the love of parents in such a short time. I've been away from home for quite a while, but tomorrow I'm going back to be with my mom and dad. I want to see them, talk to them. I cried a lot today, and now I just want to express all this through writing. No matter what you're going through, make sure to watch this series. I feel so empty today, yet it feels so fulfilling inside. I want to say thank you to every character, every team member. I've never seen anything like this before. I forgot it was just a show; I forgot these were characters…

Thank you.

I've seen a lot in life—sorrow and joy—but I have no regrets. I'm 25 years old now, and someday when I'm 50 or 60, sitting with my wife, I'll remember these moments. I just want to live—with happiness or pain, whatever it brings. I want to live my whole life, see all its ups and downs. Even if I can't give my parents happiness through money, I will surely give them love. My father is everything to me—he has worked so hard. I will stay with him. I have no complaints about my parents—only gratitude. I’m happy.

I love you, Mom and Dad. I may never be able to express it openly, but I am yours—I exist because of you. Please forgive all my mistakes, and stay happy.

Maybe I’ve written a bit too much, but if someone is still reading this—thank you. Keep your parents happy. And love your husband or wife with all your heart. Live life fully. Don't hold on to complaints or grudges…

Thank you.
– Nikhil 🤍

comment_text:
We're so busy making money, comparing ourselves with others, and all other things that don't really matter. This is what the series made me feel. Just finished it.
It’s a beautiful reminder that we only get one life, and it’s meant to be truly lived. What we do for a living helps us survive, but it’s love, art, poetry, and a sense of belonging that make us feel alive.
In a world that constantly pushes individualism, this series gently nudges you to reflect on your roots and be grateful for them. When it comes to love, being treated with respect, honesty, and care should be the foundation—not something we glorify as exceptional. There’s so much more to give and receive in love than just the bare minimum.
I’m really glad I watched it and I would highly recommend everyone to watch !!!!

comment_text:
When Life Gives You Tangerines– A Soulful Masterpiece That Stays With You

Some films don’t just tell a story – they become a part of your own. When Life Gives You Tangerines is one such rare gem. It’s been three days since I watched it, yet scenes and emotions still echo in my mind.

IU’s performance was nothing short of breathtaking. She doesn’t just act; she lives the role. From the innocence of a girl to the depth of a mother, her transformation is subtle, raw, and deeply human. The parents, too, were incredibly real – not just playing roles, but embodying what every family silently endures. Their struggles, their quiet strength, their moments of laughter amidst chaos – it felt like watching my own story unfold.

There were so many moments where I saw myself… running to my parents during tough times, seeking comfort in their simple presence. The film doesn’t dramatize life – it portrays it. With realism, honesty, and warmth.

The direction and cinematography deserve a standing ovation – every frame felt like poetry. The humor was natural, the emotions unfiltered, and the storytelling – simply unforgettable.

It’s rare for a movie to feel like a conversation with your past, your parents, your childhood, and your present fears – all at once. This was that film. A quiet, powerful reminder of how families hold each other through storms, and how sometimes, life’s sweetest moments come wrapped in the smallest things… like tangerines
 Watching from India ❤❤😂😂😂😊

comment_text:
I don’t know about ‘When Life Gives You Tangerines.’ This very depressing K drama should have been called ‘When Life Gives You Misery, Tragedy, Poverty and Death.’
Without doubt, this is the most depressing and upsetting K Drama I have ever watched. Most of the supporting cast are the most shouty and uninspiring bunch of depressives to ever grace my TV screen.
The story line, which spans three generations, jumps time frames so frequently that its almost impossible to keep track of what’s happening to whom, when and why
There are clearly some deep-seated psychological issues within Korean culture which are reflected in K Dramas, but I must say that here, within ‘Life Gives You Tangerines’ they are displayed unrelentingly.
Both IU and Park Bo Gum as the two leading actors do their best to convey the essential cruelty. tragedy and despairing nature of existence, and their life together does not bring them much joy. They are responsible for the death of one son and induce extreme resentment in their surviving son.  Their only daughter, also played by IU has a turbulent life, finally ending up in much the same position as her long-suffering mother. Also, the two actors chosen to play IU and Bo Gum in later life bear little resemblance to them as youngsters.
Finally, I would suggest you avoid this drama unless you enjoy being depressed and take all the hype with a pinch of salt.

comment_text:
This drama is a masterpiece, emphasizing what truly matters in life—unconditional love. Unlike other dramas that portray wealth and material success as the key to overcoming hardship, this story reveals a deeper purpose. Watching it, I am inspired to be like Gwan-sik for my family—to love and provide for them so they can rest, even if only for a little while. In death, we finally sleep, but there is beauty in having loved ones worth sacrificing for. That is a life truly worth living.

Spoilers ahead

Together they lived a wonderful life and achieved things people only dream of - had a family, owned a home, a boat, sent their daughter to the best uni in the country, sent her abroad, opened up their own restaurant, provided for their son and grandchildren.

video_title: When Life Gives You Tangerines | Official Trailer | Netflix
comment_text:
I hope this drama will spark your interest in Korea’s *haenyeo* (female divers) and the unique sound of their breathing, known as *sumbi sori*.  
Some people have asked, “Are all the grandmothers so rough in the way they speak?” But if you take a closer look at their lives, you’ll understand why.  
Someone once described *haenyeo* as the strongest mothers in the world.  
If you also take into account the history of Jeju Island and what’s mentioned above, you’ll enjoy the drama even more.
For reference, according to what I heard directly from a haenyeo, sumbi sori may sound beautiful, but to them, it is the sound of life—released after holding their breath for a long time.

I must say, this drama has left an indelible mark on my heart. It's undoubtedly one of the best dramas I've watched in recent times. The storyline is expertly woven, showcasing the most beautiful aspects of human relationships.

At its core, this drama is a poignant portrayal of true love. The way the characters' emotions are explored, the depth of their feelings, and the sacrifices they make for each other are all truly captivating.

What's more, the drama highlights the importance of mutual respect in relationships. The characters' interactions are laced with empathy, understanding, and a deep appreciation for one another.

The parental love depicted in the drama is also noteworthy. The sacrifices that the parents make for their children, the unconditional support they provide, and the wisdom they impart are all heartwarming.

Overall, this drama is a masterpiece that will leave you feeling hopeful, inspired, and perhaps even a little introspective. If you haven't watched it yet, I highly recommend adding it to your must-watch list!"
